hard_rules: R1: Never process transactions for sanctioned entities. R2: Never publish content without verified rights clearance. R3: Never spend above the per-transaction autonomy budget without human approval. R4: Never share customer PII outside the data residency jurisdiction. R5: Never modify the CORE Graph without human director authorization. R6: All external communications must pass brand compliance check. R7: No agent may self-promote its own authority level. R8: Financial transactions require idempotency guarantees.
rule_sources: R1: Source — OFAC/EU sanctions regulations. BOSS dimension: Regulatory. R2: Source — Copyright law + internal IP policy. BOSS dimension: Rights. R3: Source — Board-approved financial delegation policy. BOSS dimension: Financial. R4: Source — GDPR, CCPA, LGPD. BOSS dimension: Regulatory + Security. R5: Source — ADAM doctrine integrity principle. BOSS dimension: Doctrinal. R6: Source — Brand protection board mandate. BOSS dimension: Doctrinal. R7: Source — ADAM security architecture. BOSS dimension: Security. R8: Source — ADAM resilience principle. BOSS dimension: Financial + Security.
soft_expectations: E1: Respond to customer service requests within 2 hours. Exception: During major outage, extend to 8 hours with proactive communication. Cost: NPS impact. E2: All marketing campaigns reviewed by brand team before launch. Exception: Time-sensitive competitive response campaigns can launch with post-hoc review within 24 hours. Cost: Brand risk. E3: New vendor contracts require competitive bid from 3+ vendors. Exception: Sole-source allowed for <$50K or emergency operational need. Cost: Procurement efficiency. E4: Content localization in 45 languages for Tier 1 titles. Exception: Fast-follow localization acceptable for surprise viral content. Cost: Viewer experience in non-localized markets.
exception_tolerance: Default threshold: If an expectation generates exceptions >15% of the time over any rolling 30-day window, the CORE Graph Integrity Agent flags it for human review. This suggests either the expectation is unrealistic or operating conditions have changed. Directors receive a structured review packet showing: exception frequency, cost impact, root causes, and proposed doctrine adjustment options.
